description: Bot 5 levels: buffer=1.0pt + drop FIB_EXT_LO_1.272 — first slippage-aware deploy

changes_from_previous.buffer_change: BOT_ENTRY_LIMIT_BUFFER_PTS = 1.0 fixed (was target_pts/2 = 3-6pt). Tighter buffer drops the worst-fill trades (strong-rejection scenarios fill at the limit price, far below line for SELL). Lower fill rate (87.6% vs 99.4%) but better $/trade and notably smaller MaxDD.
changes_from_previous.drop_fib_ext_lo: FIB_EXT_LO_1.272 added to BOT_EXCLUDE_LEVELS. Slippage-aware backtest showed the level is structurally negative under realistic fills (-$0.99/tr at buffer=1.0pt, -$0.76/tr at target/2). 81% WR is above the 76.9% T6/S20 breakeven, but slippage tips it negative.
changes_from_previous.rationale: Selected over D variant (target/2 + drop FIB_EXT_LO at $+16.32/day, $1,544 MaxDD) for the 36% smaller drawdown profile. P&L cost is $0.66/day = ~$165/yr.

infrastructure_changes.slippage_aware_backtest: Added simulate_slippage=True mode to BacktestBroker. Tick-data limit-fill simulation, configurable latency_ms (default 100) and fill_timeout_secs (default 3.0). Fixed two bugs in the build: (1) cooldown was using time.monotonic() — wall-clock time — which meant failed-fill cooldowns NEVER expired in backtest (sim runs in seconds of wall time). Now uses sim datetime. (2) submit_bracket non-slippage path used current_price for P&L; should use line_price to match historical behavior.
infrastructure_changes.broker_race_fix: fill-during-cancel race fix (5639d76): poll parent_trade.orderStatus.status until terminal (Cancelled/ApiCancelled/Inactive/Filled) with 3s deadline, plus _has_mnq_position() backstop. Replaces the fixed 0.5s sleep that lost the race on 2026-05-05 morning.
infrastructure_changes.broker_recording_fix: Defensive flatten now waits for close fill (5s deadline), passes exit_time/exit_price to mark_open_bot_trades_orphaned which fills in pnl_usd from the row's entry_price + direction (3903596).
infrastructure_changes.vol_filter_logging: Added throttled (60s/level) skip log to the volatility filter. Was silent, made today's missed Elite alert (15:01 ET FIB_EXT_HI) impossible to diagnose without tick-level forensics (5552bee).

slippage_blind_control.note: Old fictional baseline. evaluate_bot_trade ignored fill price.

A_target_half_all_levels.note: Prior production config under realistic slippage.

B_buffer1_all_levels.note: Buffer change alone is a small regression.

D_target_half_drop_fib_ext_lo.note: Higher P&L (+$0.66/day vs C) but 57% larger MaxDD.

cap_optimization_combined.note: FAILED. Cum-table analysis was biased by selection.

experiments: buffer_sweep_v1 post-process: optimal buffer=1.0pt (+$2.93/day vs target/2 in post-process — REAL re-sim showed -$1.40/day, post-process was optimistic)
run_slippage_aware_v1: control + A target/2 + B buffer=1 + C buffer=1+drop FIB_EXT_LO
run_target2_drop_extlo: D variant validated at +$16.32/day MaxDD $1,544
run_cap_optimal: combined cap optimization (FIB_0.236 18→16, FIB_0.618 3→6, FIB_0.764 5→8, FIB_EXT_HI 6→10) = -$3.62/day vs C. Selection bias in cum-table analysis.
run_score_sweep: BOT_MIN_SCORE ∈ {-1, 0, 1, 2} all monotonically worse than no filter
approach_feature_analysis: velocity/duration/retrace/deceleration as filter candidates — best yields +$0.30/day (noise)
per_level_direction: no negative direction to drop. FIB_0.764 up is structural standout (+$4.65/tr) but can't be exploited via filter.
per_level_entry_count: cumulative $/tr by EC bucket suggested cap changes; real-sim disproved (selection bias).
key_insights: The previous +$49.60/day no-slippage baseline was overstated by ~$35/day. Real performance under slippage modeling is +$15.66/day.
All parameter-tuning levers under slippage are now exhausted: approach features, direction filter, cap optimization, score thresholds — all confirmed negative or no-signal.
Drawdowns under slippage are ~2-3x larger than no-slippage estimates (slippage compounds: smaller wins, bigger losses).
FIB_EXT_LO_1.272 is the only level with structurally negative edge under slippage (-$0.99/tr at buffer=1).
Cap optimization fails because cum-table analysis on cap-bound data has selection bias — the cap is what made the existing data look strong; raising it surfaces weaker marginal trades.

Bot at +$15.66/day vs human's +$19.18/day. The ~$3.50/day gap is structural. Closing it requires NEW DATA SOURCES (order book imbalance, tick microstructure) or a different entry mechanism (rejection-pattern recognition, hybrid pre-filter), not parameter tweaks.

Cooldowns in backtest were broken before today: time.monotonic() instead of sim time meant failed-fill cooldowns never expired during a sim day. Production was correct; only backtest was wrong. Now fixed.
Volatility filter silently skipped — added throttled logging after a missed Elite alert today couldn't be diagnosed from journals.
